content:
You sure, this isn't a bit much for a clash.
Because: you need to:

- read a map
-represent falling on a surface
-and test for connection in multiple directions, with consideration for map boundaries, and the fact that you can drop a stone in the middle of the 4score
-brute forcing multiple cases is the easier part

might take a lot of people more time than 15min, I mean there are clashes, that only require you to multiply a few input values
I think I underestimated the implementation details too much. I’ll come back with a new problem idea. Thank you for the advice.
I mean you could try to do this as an Easy Puzzle.

There seem to be simmilar puzzle already :(
Should I discard this problem?
https://www.codingame.com/training/hard/connect-four

Weirdly, this is clasified as a hard puzzle. And it only has 3 additional elements over your puzzle:
-Checking for both players
-There may be no winning move
-There may be multile winning move
Idk wait for the other guys, maybe that's enough of a difference, for putting this down as a Medium Puzzle.
I’ll remove this clash and make it an easy puzzle. Thanks!
